title: Men Are Losing the Y Chromosome with Age -- and It May Be Deadly
description: Aging men lose the Y chromosome in growing numbers of cells, linked to heart disease, cancer, Alzheimer's, and shorter lifespans, with new research showing the phenomenon is more dangerous than once believed.

title: Popular Anti-Aging Compound Dasatinib+Quercetin Causes Brain Damage in Mice
description: UConn researchers report in PNAS that the senolytic combo dasatinib+quercetin causes callosal brain damage in mice, urging caution for prophylactic prescribing and opening new avenues for understanding multiple sclerosis.

title: Inside the Longevity Boom: Where Science Ends and Fantasy Begins
description: The longevity sector attracted $8.49B in 2024 (a 220% jump) and is projected to reach $8T by 2030 per UBS, as USC researcher Valter Longo warns people are living longer but sicker.

title: NIA ITP March 2026 Results: Zero Compounds Extended Lifespan
description: The NIH Interventions Testing Program released March 2026 results showing zero new compounds extended lifespan; pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2% after 20 years of testing.

title: The Business of Longevity: Turning Aging into an Investable Market
description: Biotech labs, healthtech startups, and venture firms converge in Southern California around making longevity investable, funding platforms combining diagnostics, AI, and recurring care models.

title: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint Update: Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA
description: Bryan Johnson's updated 2026 supplement stack now takes NMN/NR six days a week, drops rapamycin entirely, and adds low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function and cellular support.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: The longevity biotech market, valued at roughly $30.7B in 2026, is projected to reach $52.8B by 2035 at 6.5% CAGR, with epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and organ regeneration advancing rapidly.

title: China Approves Stem Cell and Gene Editing Regulation Effective May 1, 2026
description: China's new biomedical technologies regulation takes effect May 1, 2026, establishing a unified framework covering stem cells, iPSCs, CAR-T, and CRISPR -- a landmark for regenerative medicine globally.

title: Blueprint Protocol 2026: Data-Driven Refinements and New Biomarkers
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint update emphasizes organ-specific metrics, real-time wearable data, and frequent lab panels, shifting from fixed rules to adaptive data-driven refinement.

title: Your Daily Coffee May Be Protecting Your Brain, 43-Year Study Finds
description: A long-term study found moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee or tea linked to 18% lower risk of dementia and better cognitive performance over time.

title: The $45B Cell Therapy Boom: Avai Bio Manufactures Alpha-Klotho Longevity Protein Cells
description: Avai Bio initiates manufacturing of a Master Cell Bank of genetically modified cells that overexpress alpha-Klotho, a key longevity protein, as the cell therapy sector hits manufacturing milestones.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033 at 20.4% CAGR
description: The longevity biotech market, valued at $12.5B in 2026, is projected to reach $45.8B by 2033, driven by AI-powered drug discovery, epigenetic clocks, and personalized longevity medicine.

title: Shanghai Biotech Unixell Wins FDA Clearance for First Stem Cell Epilepsy Therapy
description: For the first time, the FDA clears an allogeneic stem cell therapy for epilepsy to begin human testing, marking a milestone for China-based cell therapy development.

title: The Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Where the Science Still Looks Strong
description: A 2026 Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents, finding BCL-2 inhibitor ABT263 and BET inhibitor ARV825 most effective; the field is narrowing toward biomarker-guided, localized precision approaches.

title: Daily Multivitamins Slow Biological Aging in Clinical Trial
description: A two-year trial published in Nature Medicine found multivitamins slowed all five biological aging clocks by about four months, with the most benefit in those who were biologically older at baseline.

title: ARPA-H Devotes $144M to First Major Clinical Trials Targeting Aging
description: The PROSPR program awards seven teams up to $144M to build infrastructure for phase 3 trials of aging interventions, including rapamycin, semaglutide, and SGLT2 inhibitors in healthy 60-somethings.

title: US Government Funds $144M Aging Clinical Trials via PROSPR
description: Apollo Health Ventures highlights the PROSPR program as a structural shift for longevity, with portfolio company Aeovian Pharmaceuticals already in clinical studies with a rapamycin-based approach.

title: Rapamycin: The Dimmer Switch Dilemma -- Safe but Unproven in Humans
description: Comprehensive 2026 review finds low-dose rapamycin safe over one year but insufficient evidence it slows biological aging in humans; a PhenoAge model suggests possible 3.96-year biological age reduction.

title: Shingles Vaccine May Slow Biological Aging and Reduce Inflammation
description: In a national study of over 3,800 Americans aged 70+, those who received the shingles vaccine showed slower biological aging and reduced systemic inflammation.

title: Scientists Map Massive Cellular Atlas of Aging Across 21 Organs
description: Researchers analyzed nearly 7 million cells to build a cellular atlas showing aging starts earlier than expected and unfolds in a coordinated way across 21 organs throughout the body.

title: Brown University Wins $22M ARPA-H Award to Test HIV Drug Against Aging
description: The ARPA-H-funded PROSPR project will test Censavudine (TPN-101), an HIV drug, to suppress retrotransposon activity and reduce biological aging in a 48-week trial of 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65.

title: Senescence Consortium Targets Critical Biomarker Gap
description: A new consortium launches to address the lack of validated senescence biomarkers, aiming to standardize measurement tools for clinical trials of senolytic therapies.

title: Scientists Reveal Why Popular Anti-Aging Compound Spermidine May Also Fuel Cancer
description: Polyamines like spermidine boost autophagy and healthy aging via eIF5A1, but a related protein eIF5A2 drives aggressive tumor growth, revealing a double-edged longevity mechanism.

title: Young Cancer Survivors Face Faster Biological Aging and Early Dementia Risk
description: Published in Nature Communications, research shows young cancer survivors are biologically older than their chronological age, with chemotherapy accelerating the process, though exercise may help reverse it.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Mitochondrial Stress Key to Senolytics
description: Monthly roundup highlights Nature Aging findings on mitochondrial stress driving senolytic effectiveness, restoring NK cell function in Aging Cell, and SIRT6 protecting against brain protein disorders.
